prompt: Design a ForgeOps Offline Warehouse & Identity Console to run inside a browser as an English-language Svelte + TypeScript single-page application, supporting warehouse teams who need to manage inventory, outbound fulfillment, and controlled access to sensitive files and identity records without any internet connection. User roles include Administrator (sets policies, manages users and templates), Warehouse Manager (oversees inventory, waves, and discrepancies), Picker/Packer (executes assigned tasks), and Auditor (read-only access to ledgers, versions, and notification history). The UI uses a left navigation rail with frontend routing, and work is organized into Table-driven screens with filter chips, a global search bar, and contextual actions opened in a Drawer or Modal. Inventory supports multi-warehouse and sub-warehouse bins, with inbound, outbound, cycle counts, and stock transfers; safety stock defaults to 20 units per SKU per warehouse and triggers a local alert when available stock falls below the threshold. Orders reserve stock at creation and auto-release reservations after 30 minutes of inactivity or immediately on cancel, with every movement recorded in an immutable inventory ledger view including timestamp, operator, source/destination bin, quantity, and reason code. Outbound operations support wave planning (e.g., 25 lines per wave by default), task assignment to pickers, pick-path optimization using configurable zone priority then bin alphanumeric sort, and a discrepancy closed loop that requires a verification step before packing can proceed, capturing photo/file attachments and notes. File management provides chunked and resumable transfers (10 MB chunks, up to 3 concurrent chunks, and a user-set bandwidth cap such as 5 MB/s), instant upload via SHA-256 hash deduplication, versioning with rollback (retain last 10 versions), a recycle bin with 30-day retention, and local preview for common images, PDFs, text, and browser-playable audio/video formats. Face enrollment manages on-device capture from a webcam, quality checks (minimum 720p, brightness within a calibrated range, and rejection on heavy occlusion), a basic liveness flow (blink-and-turn prompt with timed frames), feature-vector generation, encrypted at-rest storage, plus grouping and bulk import/export of profiles via local JSON/CSV files. All data and logic execute entirely in the browser with no backend services: IndexedDB stores inventory tables, ledgers, file metadata, notification logs, and face templates; LocalStorage stores lightweight preferences (column layouts, last-used filters, and search history capped at 50 entries). Authentication is a local-only username/password “pseudo-login” with passwords salted and hashed (PBKDF2) and an automatic screen lock after 10 minutes idle; sensitive fields (face vectors, identity attributes, and file encryption keys) are encrypted using WebCrypto AES-GCM, and tables mask PII by default with a click-to-reveal permission. Notifications are delivered offline as in-app inbox items only, while SMS/email/official-account channels are configurable but stored as templates and queued attempts with retry rules (3 retries at 1, 5, and 15 minutes) for audit purposes rather than sent externally; users can set subscription preferences per event type (low stock, wave assigned, discrepancy opened/closed, file version rolled back) and read receipts are recorded locally. Import/export uses browser file pickers and Blob-based downloads (e.g., “Export Ledger” as CSV), ensuring administrators can back up or transfer data between devices without network dependencies, while a frontend service layer processes mock or locally imported datasets and keeps UI state consistent through centralized stores and optimistic updates with clear rollback on validation errors.
